evals.expected_output: The review should flag blocking async work, poor cancellation propagation, exception detail leakage, and weak boundary handling. It should keep style-only comments secondary or omit them.
expectations: The review flags sync-over-async caused by `.Result` on `customerApi.GetAsync`.
The review flags leaking raw exception details through `Results.BadRequest(ex.ToString())`.
The review flags missing or incorrect cancellation propagation such as passing `default` into `CreateAsync`.
The review uses severity-tagged findings rather than a single unstructured paragraph.

evals.expected_output: The review should catch early materialization, unnecessary graph loading, missing cancellation tokens, and any consistency or transactional risks around the write-plus-audit flow if justified by the code.
expectations: The review flags early materialization of all orders before filtering.
The review flags over-fetching or unnecessary `Include(o => o.Items)` for this query.
The review flags missing `CancellationToken` propagation on async EF Core calls.
The review mentions a consistency or transactional risk around `SaveChangesAsync()` followed by `auditWriter.WriteAsync(...)`, or explicitly states that the context is insufficient to confirm it.

evals.expected_output: The review should identify per-iteration HttpClient creation, missing cancellation propagation, unsafe null suppression, and possible concurrency or eventing concerns around shared mutable state.
expectations: The review flags per-iteration `new HttpClient()` creation.
The review flags missing cancellation propagation to the HTTP call and/or `Task.Delay(1000)`.
The review flags unsafe null suppression on `JsonSerializer.Deserialize(... )!`.
The review flags thread-safety or concurrency risk around updating `_cache` and raising `SyncCompleted` from the worker loop.
